title: Disposable hemolysis sensor
abstract: A disposable hemolysis sensor module (100) is described. The module comprises a flow cell (104) for housing a column of whole blood, an acoustic transducer (106) for generating acoustic forces on said flow cell for temporarily partitioning the whole blood in the flow cell into a first region comprising cell free plasma and a second region comprising blood cells, a light source (108), such as a light source comprising red LED and a yellow LED, for illuminating the cell free plasma in the first region, one or more optical imaging sensors (110), such as a camera, for acquiring one or more digital images of the cell free plasma in the first region while said cell free plasma is illuminated by said light source, and a housing (112) configured for removable installation in an instrument cartridge of a blood analysis instrument, wherein the housing is further configured to locate the flow cell, the light source and the optical imaging sensors in fixed alignment relative to each other.
claims: Claims CLAIMS 1. A module for analyzing one or more plasma analytes in whole blood comprising: a flow cell configured for housing a column of the whole blood in said module; an acoustic transducer configured for generating acoustic forces on said flow cell , the acoustic forces temporarily partitioning the whole blood in the flow cell into a first region comprising substantially cell free plasma clear of cellular components of said whole blood and a second region comprising blood cells; a light source configured to illuminate the substantially cell free plasma in the first region; one or more optical imaging sensors configured to acquire one or more digital images of the substantially cell free plasma in the first region while said substantially cell free plasma is illuminated by said light source; and a housing configured for removable installation in an instrument cartridge, wherein the housing is further configured to locate the flow cell, the light source and the optical imaging sensors in fixed alignment relative to each other. 2. The module of claim 1, further comprising: memory configured to store said digital images; processor circuitry in communication with said memory, said processor circuitry configured to analyze the digital images to characterize one or more analytes in said substantially cell free plasma. 3. The module of claim 1 wherein said flow cell further comprises a lumen and walls surrounding the lumen. 4. The module of claim 1 wherein said substantially cell free plasma is localized in the lumen of the flow cell and the cellular components of said whole blood are localized at the walls of the flow cell. 5. The module of claim 1 wherein said flow cell further comprises an inlet and an outlet for the whole blood. 6. The module of claim 1 further comprising an electrical connector configured to deliver electrical power and control signals to and from the module. 7. The module of claim 1 wherein said module is configured for removable installation in one or more instruments. 8. The module of claim 1 wherein said one or more analytes is free hemoglobin. 9. The module of claim 1 wherein said light source comprises one or more light emitting diodes (LEDs). 10. The module of claim 9 wherein said one or more LEDs are multicolored. 11. The module of claim 10 wherein said multicolored LEDs are selected from the group consisting of yellow and red colors or a combination thereof wherein said red color has a wavelength of about 570 nanometers and said yellow color has a wavelength of about 610 nanometers. 12. The module of claim 9 wherein a light pipe is configured between the LEDs and the flow cell, wherein said light pipe facilitates homogenization of the LED colors. 13. The module of claim 1 wherein the processing circuitry is configured to execute a glass correction algorithm to compensate for any fluctuations in LED intensity. 14. The module of claim 1 wherein said imaging sensor is a camera comprising an image sensor board and imaging lenses. 15. The module of claim 14 wherein said camera further comprising an optical aperture installed at the imaging lenses for controlling optical aberrations. 16. The module of claim 1 wherein the light source further comprising a diffuser element for uniformly illuminating the flow cell. 17. The module of claim 1 wherein said module is disposable. 18. The module of claim 2, wherein the processing circuitry is configured to execute an image processing algorithm, the image processing algorithm generating a histogram of pixel values for each of the one or more digital images. 19. The module of claim 18, wherein the histogram is representative of an absorption spectra of the one or more plasma analytes. 20. The module of claim 20, wherein the histogram is representative of concentration of the one or more plasma analytes.
